description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service, defines BOOTSTRAP_VERSION and HUGO_VERSION variables, and includes the stages build-base, build-images, test, and release. Add a global before_script that runs docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com for all jobs. In the build-base stage add a build-base job that executes docker build --pull -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:base -f Dockerfile-base . and then docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:base. In the build-images stage add a hidden .build-bleed job that runs docker build --pull -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:bleed-$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME -f Dockerfile-bleed . and docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:bleed-$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME, and also a build-release job that runs docker build --pull -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME --build-arg HUGO_VERSION=$HUGO_VERSION -f Dockerfile . followed by docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME. In the release stage add a release-bleed job (only on the master branch) that pulls the bleed image, retags it as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:bleed, and pushes it, and a release-image job (only on master) that pulls $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME, tags it as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$HUGO_VERSION, $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest, and $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:release, and pushes the latest and release tags.
